analysis.reasoning: Core message: Lavrov jokes that the US cannot have a color revolution because it lacks an American Embassy—implying the US orchestrates such uprisings abroad—while feigning concern that the US not fall into crisis. Evaluation by narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile/decadent): Yes. The quip insinuates US interference in other countries’ internal affairs via its embassies, portraying the US as a hostile, subversive actor; the ironic 'we would not want ... to fall into a deep crisis' underscores moral critique. RU2 (Russia leads an alternative system): Not present; no mention of multipolarity, BRICS, or alternative frameworks. RU3 (Russia benevolent partner): Partially implied by the performative concern for US stability, but there is no concrete claim of partnership or constructive action, so not supported. RU4 (Russian history admirable): Not relevant. RU5 (Russia at the forefront): Not relevant. RU6 (Russia culture/nature): Not relevant. Conclusion: The tweet promotes RU1.
